title: Typical Protocol for NEBExpress GamS Nuclease Inhibitor when used with the NEBExpress Cell-free E. coli Protein Synthesis System (NEB #E5360)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fkmg7","text":"NEBExpress GamS Nuclease Inhibitor is a recombinant protein that inhibits Exonuclease V (RecBCD) activity and stabilizes linear DNA templates in E. coli  based in vitro  protein synthesis reactions.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":145,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":160,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c2530","text":"Enhances synthesis yield of linear DNA using the NEBExpress Cell-freeE. coliProtein Synthesis System","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":69,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"20pmj","text":"Recombinant enzyme; ≥ 95% purity, as determined by SDS-PAGE ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8r4au","text":"No detectable protease activity, ensuring stability of desired target protein","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3om4q","text":"Compatible with various target proteins, ranging from 17 to 230 kDa","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b3bae","text":"Optimal storage buffer for performance in protein synthesis reactions","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9t9g1","text":"Flexible reaction conditions achieve maximum yield; activity can be sustained for 10 hours at 37°C or up to 24 hours at lower temperatures","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

GamS Nuclease Inhibitor is a recombinant protein that inhibits Exonuclease V (RecBCD) activity and stabilizes linear DNA templates in E. coli  based in vitro  protein synthesis reactions.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":145,"length":9},{"style":"italic","offset":160,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3la1t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3lfk1","text":"Figure 1: Protein synthesis of a linear DNA template using the NEBExpress® Cell-free E. coli  Protein Synthesis System supplemented with NEBExpress GamS Nuclease Inhibitor","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":171},{"style":"italic","offset":85,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dnv9c","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"7g8no","text":"50 µl reactions containing 100 ng linear template DNA, the components of the NEBExpress® Cell-free E. coli Protein Synthesis System and 1.5 µg NEBExpress GamS Nuclease Inhibitor incubated for 5 hours at 37°C were monitored for activity as determined by fluorescence signal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":273}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"84id9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9gp73","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eqd0d","text":"NEBExpress® Cell-free E. coli  Protein Synthesis System","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":55},{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":55}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ir5n","text":"The NEBExpress® Cell-free E. coli  Protein Synthesis System is a coupled transcription/translation system designed to synthesize proteins encoded by a DNA template under the control of a T7 RNA Polymerase promoter. The system offers high expression levels, the ability to produce high molecular weight proteins, scalability, and is cost-effective for high throughput expression applications.  The speed and robustness of the system facilitates protein synthesis in applications such as protein engineering, mutagenesis studies and enzyme screening. In addition, it can be used to generate proteins for biophysical and structure-function analyses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":26,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"abobr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"295af","text":"The NEBExpress® Cell-free E. coli  Protein Synthesis System contains all the components required for protein synthesis, except for the target template DNA. It is a combination of a highly active cell extract from a genetically engineered strain, a reaction buffer, and an optimized T7 RNA Polymerase, which together yield robust expression of a wide variety of protein targets ranging from 17 to 230 KDa.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":26,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"78std","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a6ckr","text":"Protein synthesis is achieved with a short incubation and synthesized protein is compatible with downstream purification or analysis by SDS-PAGE, Western Blot or direct functional assay. The novel formulation of this system allows samples to be loaded directly onto SDS-PAGE, without the need for acetone or TCA precipitation.  Additionally, synthesized protein can be isolated from the reaction mixture using affinity purification techniques, such as immobilized metal affinity chromatography (IMAC), for further structural and/or functional characterization.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d3957","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ag187","text":"Figure 1: Protein synthesis using the NEBExpress®Cell-free E. coli  Protein Synthesis System","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":92},{"style":"italic","offset":59,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2hosk","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"5c28q","text":"50 µl reactions containing 250 ng template DNA were incubated at 37°C for 3 hours. 2 µl of each reaction were analyzed by SDS-PAGE using a 10–20% Tris-glycine gel. The red dot indicates the protein of interest. M = Unstained Protein Standard, Broad range (NEB#P7717), “Neg” = negative control, no DNA.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":301}],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":259,"length":6}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"9D50B069A50911EA82330A58A9FEAC2A","height":null,"id":65801,"is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":null,"placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/public/267c32e26e8857d37315dd983e32caac261c928f925407a9565bc015a1fe4b9e/caik9nan.jpg","source":"https://content.protocols.io/public/267c32e26e8857d37315dd983e32caac261c928f925407a9565bc015a1fe4b9e/caij9nan.jpg","width":null}},"1":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"E6C627A0A50911EA82330A58A9FEAC2A","height":null,"id":65803,"is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":null,"placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/public/267c32e26e8857d37315dd983e32caac261c928f925407a9565bc015a1fe4b9e/cain9nan.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/public/267c32e26e8857d37315dd983e32caac261c928f925407a9565bc015a1fe4b9e/caim9nan.png","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/p7717-unstained-protein-standard-broad-range-10-200-kda","width":null}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"E6C62686A50911EA82330A58A9FEAC2A","url":"https://www.neb.com/products/p7717-unstained-protein-standard-broad-range-10-200-kda"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ccked","text":"Using a positive control template to verify protein synthesis can be useful when unfamiliar with in vitro transcription-translation protocols. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dmpnc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6nj33","text":"To prevent nuclease contamination, wear gloves and use nuclease-free tubes and tips. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8vaet","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"53hn5","text":"Reactions are typically 50 μl but can be scaled down or up, as needed. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fn7e1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ai0ft","text":"Reactions are typically assembled in nuclease-free 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tubes. Components can be pre-assembled to create a master mix for a desired number of reactions. Use the master mix immediately, discard any unused master mix.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Gently vortex the NEBExpress S30 Synthesis Extract and Protein Synthesis Buffer to mix.

Combine reagents in a 1.5 ml microcentrifuge tube On ice as follows: %s %s During the experimental setup, it is recommended to add the linear DNA template in the last step to allow GamS to bind and inhibit RecBCD exonuclease before RecBCD has a chance to act on the DNA. Optimal GamS concentration must be determined empirically for a particular target.
Incubate reactions at 37 °C , with shaking, for 02:00:00 – 04:00:00 . Additional incubation time (maximum 10 hours) at 37°C may increase yield.
Analyze by method of choice or freeze at -20 °C for later use.
creator: New England Biolabs Tech Support
